title: Utrecht Gender Dysphoria Scale – Gender Spectrum (UGDS-GS)
description: A measure of gender dysphoria and gender affirmation across gender identities.
author: McGuire et al. (2019)

instructions.title: Instructions
instructions.text: Please indicate the extent to which you agree with each statement. “Assigned sex” refers to the sex assigned at birth. “Affirmed gender” refers to the gender you identify with.

questions.text: I prefer to behave in ways that are typical of my affirmed gender.
When I am treated as my assigned sex, I feel distressed.
I feel good when I live as my affirmed gender.
I want others to treat me as my affirmed gender.
Living as my affirmed gender feels more natural than living as my assigned sex.
I feel unhappy when I have to behave as my assigned sex.
I feel uncomfortable experiencing sexual feelings in relation to my assigned sex.
Going through puberty felt like a betrayal to me.
My physical development during puberty was distressing.
I wish I had been born as my affirmed gender.
Physical characteristics of my assigned sex cause me distress.
My life would feel meaningless if I had to live as my assigned sex.
I feel hopeless about having to live as my assigned sex.
I feel upset when others use the wrong gendered terms for me.
I feel unhappy because my body reflects my assigned sex.
I feel strong dislike toward my assigned sex.
I feel uncomfortable behaving as my assigned sex.
I sometimes feel that it would be better not to live than to live as my assigned sex.
